_description: Session lifecycle log. One entry per session. Append only.
author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net

sessions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
sessions.task: Analyze US-001 User Authentication and Session Management — Phases 1 (Requirements Discovery) and 2 (Feasibility & Impact) only; stop for approval before generating BRD/US/plan.
sessions.goal: Requirements discovery + feasibility/impact analysis for the NEXA authentication story, greenfield.
accomplished: Mapped FR-AA-001/002/003/004/012/013 from functional-spec.md and BR-006/BR-018 from business-rules.md.
Extracted concrete NFR-SEC-001 auth parameters from design-doc.md (Argon2id mem>=64MB/iter>=3, JWT 15min access + refresh rotation, 30-min inactivity, lockout 5/15min).
Confirmed stack: .NET 10 Core Monolith Auth Module (/src/Nexa.Auth/), React18/Next.js14 SPA, Azure SQL + EF Core 8, APIM JWT, SendGrid for reset email, Key Vault secrets.
discoveries: Codebase is GREENFIELD: no src/, apps/, docs/, or .sln exist. US-001 bootstraps the solution.
TRACEABILITY ERROR: backlog AC-004 cites 'Argon2id per SAD ADR-005' but ADR-005 is Roslyn analyzers. Argon2id source is tech-doc technical constraint + NFR-SEC-001 + design-doc security table.
JWT signing algorithm inconsistent across docs: design-doc says RS256; SAD Auth Module says HMAC-signed; tech-doc env var Jwt__SigningKeySecretName implies single symmetric key.
Account lockout (5 failures/15min progressive) required by NFR-SEC-001 + design-doc but NOT covered by any US-001 acceptance criterion.
Password policy (length/complexity) referenced across docs ('platform's password policy') but never concretely defined.
Reset-link TTL and single-use semantics not specified for FR-AA-012.

Architectural tension: tech-doc says only Dispatch Module may call providers, but traceability maps FR-AA-012 email to 'Auth Service / IEmailSender Adapter'. Transactional-email path for auth (activation + reset) needs a confirmed mechanism.
Scope overlap: US-025 (Login Screen + Remember-me/FR-AA-011) owns the auth UI; US-001 owns backend auth + session. Boundary must be explicit.
open_items: Awaiting user approval to proceed to BRD/User Story/Implementation Plan generation.
6 clarification questions pending (password policy, lockout scope, JWT algo, reset-link TTL, transactional email path, US-025 boundary).
sessions.next_session_hint: US-001 analysis Phases 1-2 delivered and awaiting approval. On resume: if approved, generate BRD + User Story + Implementation Plan for backend auth (Auth Module in Core Monolith). Resolve the 6 clarification questions first; cite Argon2id from tech-doc/NFR-SEC-001 NOT ADR-005.

sessions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
sessions.task: US-001: after coordinator approval + 6 resolved clarifications, execute Phases 3-5 (Planning, Acceptance Criteria/UX, generate deliverables).
sessions.goal: Produce BRD, User Story, 3 ADRs, and Implementation Plan for US-001 backend authentication.
discoveries: UX_Booster skipped: US-001 has no UI surface (login UI is US-025) per Phase 4.5 / skill Step 1.5 skip rule.
Added AC-007 (account lockout 5/15min) per coordinator; original backlog had only AC-001..AC-006.
Story points 13, complexity Very High, ~110h realistic due to greenfield bootstrap.
code-standards §3.1.7 confirms Argon2id via Konscious.Security.Cryptography (mem>=64MB shown, iter=3); bcrypt/PBKDF2/MD5/SHA prohibited.
open_items: PO to confirm/override password policy defaults.
Tech Lead/Security to confirm refresh-token absolute lifetime.
Consider splitting a Solution Bootstrap enabler story (greenfield effort risk R-002).
Next: WorkItem_Operations skill can consume the User Story to create the US-001 work item (not done here).
sessions.next_session_hint: US-001 deliverables (BRD/US/3 ADRs/IP/CHANGELOG) are complete under docs/. Next logical step is WorkItem_Operations to create the tracker item, then Coder implements per IP-2026-07-20. Remember: RS256 (not HMAC), Argon2id from code-standards/NFR-SEC-001 not ADR-005, auth email direct via IEmailSender.
